full_response:
Let  

m_h ≡ Higgs rest–mass /c^2               m_h c^2 = 125 GeV  
E_h ≡ total energy of the Higgs in the lab (c = 1)  
p_h ≡ |p⃗ _h| = √(E_h² – m_h²)                        β ≡ p_h /E_h  

Place the laboratory \(z\)-axis along the Higgs momentum.  
After the decay \(h\to\gamma\gamma\) pick the photon whose lab–energy is measured to be \(E_\gamma\) and let θ be the angle it makes with the +z axis (the original Higgs direction).

1.  Work in the Higgs rest frame (“*”):
    • The two photons are back-to-back and have  
            E* = p* = m_h /2.  
    • Let photon 1 be emitted at polar angle θ* with respect to the z-axis.

2.  Boost to the lab with velocity β ẑ.  
    For any particle,
        E = γ(E* + β p* cos θ*),           
        cos θ = (cos θ* + β)/(1 + β cos θ*)             (velocity-addition).

   With E* = p*,

        E_γ = γ E* (1 + β cos θ*)                                (1)

   Solve the aberration formula for cos θ* and insert into (1):

        cos θ* = (cos θ – β)/(1 – β cos θ),

        E_γ = γ E* (1 – β²)/(1 – β cos θ).

   Because 1 – β² = 1/γ² and γ = E_h /m_h,

        E_γ =  (E*/γ) · 1/(1 – β cos θ)
             = (m_h/2)(m_h/E_h) · 1/(1 – β cos θ)
             = m_h² /(2E_h) · 1/(1 – β cos θ).                   (2)

3.  Rearrange (2) to obtain cos θ:

        1 – β cos θ = m_h² /(2E_h E_γ)

        β cos θ     = 1 – m_h² /(2E_h E_γ)

        cos θ       = [1 – m_h² /(2E_h E_γ)] / β
                    = [E_h – m_h² /(2E_γ)] / p_h                ( because βE_h = p_h ).        (3)

Therefore

θ = arccos [ (E_h – m_h² /(2E_γ)) / √(E_h² – m_h²) ].

(Equation (3) is often the most convenient form.)

Domain check: as θ → 0, cos θ → 1 gives the maximum photon energy  
E_γ,max = (E_h/2)(1 + β) = (E_h + p_h)/2;  
as θ → π, cos θ → –1 gives the minimum photon energy  
E_γ,min = (E_h/2)(1 – β) = (E_h – p_h)/2.

Boxed result:

\boxed{\,\displaystyle \cos\theta \;=\; \frac{E_h-\dfrac{m_h^{\,2}}{2E_\gamma}}{\sqrt{E_h^{\,2}-m_h^{\,2}}}\; ,\qquad
\theta = \arccos\!\left[\frac{E_h-\dfrac{m_h^{\,2}}{2E_\gamma}}{\sqrt{E_h^{\,2}-m_h^{\,2}}}\right]\,}

Confidence: 83%
